meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Lionel Messi scores twice as Inter Miami defeat Rapids in MLS
items.summary: Argentine forward's brace included the match winner against Colorado Rapids in front of over 75,000 fans in Denver.

items.title: Fire engulfs more than 200 homes in Malaysian floating village
items.summary: A huge fire tore through the floating village of Kampung Bahagia in Sabah, Malaysia, destroying more than 200 homes.

items.title: Cash shortages grip Yemen despite currency stabilisation
items.summary: Cash-strapped Yemenis face rising frustration as exchange firms limit currency conversion amid ongoing liquidity crisis.

items.title: Bulgarians head to polls for eighth time in five years
items.summary: Bulgaria votes to elect a new parliament after mass protests brought down the previous government in December.

items.title: Why Britain’s far-right celebrates a saint revered in Palestine
items.summary: Hundreds of far-right “Britain First” supporters marched in the streets of Manchester to celebrate Saint George.

items.title: LeBron, Lakers shock Rockets with Game 1 win in NBA playoffs
items.summary: Despite missing leading scorers Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, the LA Lakers and LeBron James defeat Houston in opener.

items.title: Real Sociedad upset Atletico Madrid to clinch fourth Copa del Rey title
items.summary: Real win the final of Spain's premier annual knockout football competition with a dramatic penalty shootout in Seville.

items.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles towards sea off its east coast
items.summary: Multiple ballistic missiles fired from eastern Sinpo area in seventh such test this year.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran says no date set for US talks, Hormuz Strait closed
items.summary: IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the US stops blockading Iranian ports.

items.title: Trinidad and Tobago police uncover 56 bodies, mostly children, at cemetery
items.summary: Police suspect the grim discovery in the city of Cumuto may be a case of 'unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses'.

items.title: Iran reasserts control of Hormuz Strait as Trump warns against ‘blackmail’
items.summary: Top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf says US naval blockade of Iran's ports is 'a clumsy and ignorant decision'.

items.title: Manchester United beat Chelsea as Cunha strike boosts Champions League hope
items.summary: Manchester United strengthen grip on third spot in Premier League with 1-0 win that leaves Chelsea relying on favours.

items.title: Iran military orders Indian ship to abort Strait of Hormuz passage
items.summary: Video shows the moment Iran’s military ordered an Indian ship to abort its passage of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Mexico, Spain and Brazil call for Cuba’s sovereignty to be protected
items.summary: The three countries pledge more aid to Cuba and say Cubans must decide their own future amid a US pressure campaign.

items.title: Israel says established a ‘yellow line’ in Lebanon, as it has in Gaza
items.summary: It is the first time Israel has referred to such a 'yellow line' in Lebanon, after using a similar measure in Gaza.

items.title: Will Keir Starmer resign?
items.summary: UK PM says he was unaware Mandelson failed security clearance.

items.title: Mazzucato on the Iran war’s economic shock: Who pays the price?
items.summary: Redi Tlhabi speaks to economist Mariana Mazzucato on the Iran war's economic fallout and who’s really paying the price.

items.title: Mexico arrests suspected Hungarian drug trafficker amid crime crackdown
items.summary: Fugitive Janos Balla, 48, was arrested in Quintana Roo as President Claudia Sheinbaum seeks to increase cartel arrests.

items.title: Sudanese refugees trapped between borders and bureaucracy in Morocco
items.summary: A growing number of Sudanese refugees are arriving into Morocco after fleeing war, but find themselves stuck.

items.title: Ukraine police shoot dead gunman who killed six in Kyiv, took hostages
items.summary: The attacker has been identified as a "58-year-old Moscow man", but no motive has been established.

items.title: Trump joined by Joe Rogan as he signs order to speed up psychedelic review
items.summary: Order calls on federal government to relax restrictions on psychedelics, including ibogaine, for potential treatments.

items.title: ‘No regrets’: Venezuela’s Machado defends giving Nobel medal to Trump
items.summary: Maria Corina Machado gave Trump her Nobel Peace Prize after the US leader captured Nicolas Maduro.

items.title: Chelsea 0-1 Manchester United: Premier League – as it happened
items.summary: All our updates, with full post-match coverage, reaction and interviews, following our text commentary stream.

items.title: “Lords of war.” Brazil’s president condemns UN Security Council
items.summary: Brazilian president has described the five permanent members of the UN Security Council as “Lords of War".

items.title: French soldier serving with UNIFIL killed in Lebanon attack
items.summary: The deadly incident comes just days after Israel and Lebanon announced a 10-day ceasefire.
